title: Intent/Subnet spec
description: Desired a subnet (a CIDR range) declared in Git (ADR-0059, a named-singleton provisioning Intent). NOT an Entity (§1.2): cardinality-1 — the ONE named Entity that SHOULD exist, correlated desired<->built by (intentKind, name). The reconcile surfaces a GATED build (§5 Flow 1), never auto-launches, never writes the unbuilt. Landscape-agnostic: 'builder' is the swap point; 'params' is opaque, validated only against the builder's own input Contract (§1.5) — no per-provider network fields here (§1.1, no universal ontology). The built infra's attributes live in an owned-but-uncovered Facet namespace until a Contract consumes them (decision 1); this schema types only the CaC declaration seam, never the graph Facet.

projectKind.description: The Entity kind the built infra projects AS (default subnet), so Views select it. Carried on the build's Run-provenance projection — NEVER written by the reconcile (§1.2).

labels.description: Selection labels stamped on the built Entity's projection (per-key owned, ADR-0041).

builder.description: The build Actuator/Action ref (crossplane | opentofu | dns | vsphere-network | ...). The landscape swap point (§1.4/§1.5); core learns no provider.

buildWorkflow.description: The GATED Workflow the operator launches to build the singleton (Gate -> builder -> project-back). Referenced by the provisioning Finding; never auto-launched (§5 Flow 1).

params.description: Opaque build params passed to the builder, validated against ITS input Contract downstream (§1.5). Never typed here (§1.1).

placement.description: Optional desired topology placement (ADR-0059 decision 5): a Relation cannot be declared in Git (§1.2), so placement's CaC home is the Intent; the build projects the placed-in edge (Run-provenance). Divergence from the observed placement raises a placement-drift Finding (§1.8).

subnet.description: The subnet Entity name this unit is placed in (an Intent/Subnet name).

zone.description: The dmz / availability-zone Entity name this unit sits in.
